title: localization — validation rules
description: Custom Laravel validation rules published by LocalizationServiceProvider::boot() so any FormRequest / Data class can validate translation blobs. All three rules operate on the JSONB shape `{ locale_code: { field: value } }` written by the `HasTranslations` trait.

rules.name: translatable_required
rules.description: Ensures the JSONB blob contains a NON-EMPTY value for every locale in the argument list. Argument is either a comma-joined list of BCP-47 codes or the sentinel `tenant_required` (resolved at runtime from `TenantLocale where is_required=true`).
rules.default_message: The :attribute must have a non-empty value for locale :locale.

rules.name: translatable_locale_available
rules.description: Ensures every locale key in the JSONB blob is (a) a valid BCP-47 tag AND (b) enabled on the current scope. Scope defaults to `tenant` (rejects locales not enabled on the caller's TenantLocale); `platform` allows any `PlatformLanguage.locale_code` (used by platform-admin surfaces).
rules.default_message: The :attribute contains a translation for locale :locale which is not enabled for this tenant.

rules.name: translatable_length_per_locale
rules.description: Enforces per-locale character length limits on the JSONB blob. Rejects the whole request when ANY locale value exceeds `<max>`. Validates against multibyte character counts (mb_strlen), not raw bytes, so `é` counts as 1 character not 2.
rules.default_message: The :attribute for locale :locale may not be longer than :max characters (got :length).

usage_from_data_class: The HasTranslations trait's Rules() method auto-generates the FormRequest rule set from #[Translatable] attributes on the model, so most callers never write these rules manually. This file is the canonical registry for the underlying rule implementations.
